title: Under pressure, lobes tells you the cortex model is busy and to retry shortly — it never silently degrades your request onto a weaker or different-capability model.

claims.text: Under pressure, lobes tells you the cortex model is busy and to retry shortly — it never silently degrades your request onto a weaker or different-capability model.

honesty_conditions.text: On a live pressured fleet, a cortex request observably returns a retryable busy signal (not a Gemma/other-model completion), and a retrying client eventually receives a genuine cortex answer once pressure clears.

claims.text: Callers of the lobes gateway generate lane — the Culture mesh acp vllm-local provider and colleague — plus operators running the memory/swap-pressured GB10 fleet.

honesty_conditions.text: The acp vllm-local provider and colleague are the actual pressured-fleet callers of the generate lane (they dial the gateway origin per #87), so THEIR retry behavior is what determines whether busy backpressure is safe in practice.

claims.text: A cortex/main request under swap/iowait pressure gets an explicit, retryable 'busy' response (HTTP 503 + Retry-After) instead of a silent 2xx answer from a substituted cheaper or different-capability model.

hard_questions.text: HTTP 503 (server overloaded, matches OpenAI 'engine is overloaded') vs 429 (rate-limited) — which status best fits 'the model is busy, retry shortly' for our OpenAI-compatible callers?

claims.text: Under pressure the policy caps the tier to minor; with minor unwired the router's upward-fallback silently serves the request from Gemma (multimodal) — a different capability — disclosed only via X-Lobes-Tier headers (bug #85).

honesty_conditions.text: The silent Gemma substitution is the real current code path, not a hypothetical: resolve_tier_request('cortex', swap=80%) on a default fleet with minor unwired returns served=Gemma, tier=minor, reason=pressure (reproduced against the live code).

claims.text: A caller asking cortex for authoritative reasoning must never silently receive a weaker or wrong-capability answer; an honest 'busy, retry' preserves the capability contract (#81) and lets clients back off instead of acting on a degraded result.

honesty_conditions.text: For a cortex caller, a wrong-capability/degraded answer is materially worse than a retry delay — callers would rather wait and retry than silently act on a Gemma answer to an authoritative-reasoning request.

claims.text: Not a request queue/scheduler or admission-control rewrite, and not a change to vLLM's internal batching; the swap/iowait sampling and tunable thresholds (#86) stay as the pressure signal — only the RESPONSE to pressure changes.

honesty_conditions.text: The change is confined to the gateway response/decision path (handle_post + pressure-policy decision); the swap/iowait sampler (_pressure.py) and the threshold env vars (#86) are untouched.

claims.text: On a pressured fleet with no minor gear, a cortex request returns 503 busy+Retry-After (never a Gemma completion), and a retrying client eventually gets a real cortex answer once pressure clears.

Under pressure, the gateway returns HTTP 503 with a Retry-After header and an OpenAI-shaped error body for a generate request whose requested tier can't be honored, instead of rewriting it onto another model.

honesty_conditions.text: OpenAI-compatible clients (the acp vllm-local provider, colleague, and generic SDKs) treat a 503 + Retry-After on POST /v1/chat/completions as a retryable transient, not a fatal error surfaced to the end user.

hard_questions.text: risk: If a client does NOT retry on 503 (treats it as fatal), turning off degrade makes lobes strictly less available under pressure than today's always-answer-with-something behavior — a real regression for non-retrying callers.

claims.text: The legacy degrade-to-minor behavior becomes selectable, not removed: a pressure-policy MODE (e.g. LOBES_PRESSURE_POLICY=busy|degrade) picks busy-backpressure vs the old silent degrade; the shipped DEFAULT flips to busy.

hard_questions.text: Does the user want the silent degrade path REMOVED outright, or kept as an opt-in mode with busy as the new default? The announcement says 'turn off degrading', which could mean either.

claims.text: The busy response carries a disclosure header (X-Lobes-Tier-Reason: busy) plus Retry-After so a client can distinguish transient busy (retry) from the existing hard 502 'all backends unavailable' (don't retry).

honesty_conditions.text: A client can reliably distinguish busy (retry) from the existing 502 all-backends-unavailable (don't retry) from status+header alone, and on the streaming path the busy status is emitted before any SSE body bytes.

claims.text: Busy backpressure applies only within the generate/cortex capability lane; it never substitutes across capabilities. A request that can be honored on its own healthy gear is still served — busy is returned only when the requested capability itself can't be served under the current tier ceiling.
The mesh acp vllm-local provider and colleague treat 503+Retry-After as a transient/retryable signal and back off, rather than surfacing it as a hard failure to the end user.

lobes status --pressure and the gateway /capabilities/GET status surface the busy policy state (mode=busy, current tier ceiling, whether requests are being shed) so operators can see WHY a client is being told to wait.

honesty_conditions.text: lobes status --pressure and the gateway status endpoint report mode=busy and the current tier ceiling WITHOUT issuing a live generate request, and the reported ceiling matches the admission decision handle_post would actually make.

claims.text: The busy trigger reuses the EXISTING swap/iowait pressure signal (mode=degraded becomes mode=busy): when the policy would have degraded, it sheds instead. It does NOT introduce vLLM queue-depth (waiting-count) admission control.

hard_questions.text: Should 'busy' key on the swap/iowait pressure signal (what degrade uses today) or on real vLLM queue depth (the busy:{running,waiting} already probed for /status)? Pressure means 'box is thrashing'; queue-depth means 'this model is saturated' — different triggers.

claims.text: A cortex OR senses request under swap/iowait pressure gets an explicit, retryable busy response (HTTP 429 + Retry-After) instead of a silent 2xx answer from a substituted cheaper or different-capability model.

honesty_conditions.text: On the shed path the gateway issues NO upstream model request — it returns 429 itself — so a busy cortex/senses request provably never reaches a substitute model.

claims.text: Under pressure, the gateway returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header and an OpenAI-shaped error body for a generate/multimodal request it would otherwise have substituted onto a different model, instead of rewriting it onto that model.

honesty_conditions.text: The acp vllm-local provider, colleague, and generic OpenAI SDKs treat 429 + Retry-After on POST /v1/chat/completions as a retryable transient with backoff, not a fatal error surfaced to the end user.

hard_questions.text: risk: If a caller does NOT honor 429/Retry-After (treats it as fatal), removing degrade makes lobes strictly less available under pressure than today's always-answer-with-something behavior — a real regression for non-retrying callers, and the reason client retry handling is a honesty condition, not an assumption.

claims.text: The silent degrade-to-minor path is REMOVED outright: no 'degrade' mode and no LOBES_PRESSURE_POLICY toggle to restore it. Under pressure the only behavior is busy backpressure — which makes the cross-capability substitution of #85 structurally impossible.

Busy applies to ANY request pressure would otherwise substitute — both cortex (main/hard) and senses (multimodal/normal). The gateway never serves a different model than requested under pressure; a request whose own gear is healthy and served-as-requested is unaffected.

An explicit model=minor request is still served under pressure (served as requested, not a substitution). Busy is returned only for a request whose requested tier/capability pressure would otherwise have swapped for a different model.

The mesh acp vllm-local provider and colleague treat a 429 + Retry-After on /v1/chat/completions as a transient/retryable signal and back off, rather than surfacing it as a hard failure to the end user.
On a pressured fleet with no minor gear, a cortex request returns 429 busy+Retry-After (never a Gemma completion), and a retrying client eventually gets a real cortex answer once pressure clears.

honesty_conditions.text: Success is observable end-to-end (not just a unit-tested decision()): a scripted pressured request shows 429+Retry-After for cortex, and after pressure clears a 200 from the real cortex model.

open_vagueness.text: Exact Retry-After seconds value and whether it's static or derived from pressure severity / observed queue drain time.
